description:
You are the database administrator of a small development server.  
  A copy of the PostgreSQL configuration has been placed in the project
  directory so that you can safely test tuning changes without touching
  the real instance.

  1. File that must be modified  
     • Path : /home/user/db/postgresql.conf  
     • Current relevant excerpt (already present in the file):

       ```
       # PostgreSQL configuration file
       # Memory settings
       work_mem = 4MB
       # End
       ```

  2. Required change  
     • Increase the value of the parameter `work_mem` from **4MB** to
       **16MB**.  
     • The line must keep exactly the same syntax spacing as the
       original, except for the new value (i.e. it must become  
       `work_mem = 16MB`).

  3. Logging requirement  
     • After you finish editing the configuration, create a log file
       called /home/user/logs/work_mem_update.log.  
     • The log file must contain exactly **one single line** with the
       fields separated by the pipe character “|” (U+007C).  
     • Fixed format that the automated test will read:

       ```
       2024-01-01 00:00:00|work_mem|4MB|16MB|UPDATED
       ```
       (Five fields: static timestamp, parameter name, old value,
       new value, literal word “UPDATED”. No extra spaces, no
       newline at the end.)

  4. Success criteria  
     The automated checker will confirm both of the following:
     • /home/user/db/postgresql.conf now contains the line
       `work_mem = 16MB` (exactly, with the same leading spacing as
       the original).  
     • /home/user/logs/work_mem_update.log exists and contains
       exactly the single line shown in section 3.

  You are free to use any standard GNU/Linux command-line tools, but
  everything should be achievable with no more than 2–3 shell
  commands.  Once these conditions are met, you have completed the
  task.
truth:
Initial filesystem state (before the agent starts):

  • Directory /home/user/db exists with permissions 755 and contains a
    single file postgresql.conf whose exact contents are:

    (BEGIN)
/home/user/db/postgresql.conf
-----------------------------
# PostgreSQL configuration file
# Memory settings
work_mem = 4MB
# End
(END)

  • Directory /home/user/logs exists, permissions 755, and is empty.

  Desired final state (after the agent finishes):

  A) /home/user/db/postgresql.conf must contain **exactly**:

    (BEGIN)
/home/user/db/postgresql.conf
-----------------------------
# PostgreSQL configuration file
# Memory settings
work_mem = 16MB
# End
(END)

  B) /home/user/logs/work_mem_update.log must exist, permissions 644,
     and contain **no trailing newline**.  Exact byte sequence:

     323032342d30312d30312030303a30303a30307c776f726b5f6d656d7c344d427c31364d427c55504441544544
     (hex-encoded form of  
      “2024-01-01 00:00:00|work_mem|4MB|16MB|UPDATED”)

  The testing harness will:

  • cat /home/user/db/postgresql.conf | grep -Fx "work_mem = 16MB"
  • cmp -b /home/user/logs/work_mem_update.log \
          <(printf '2024-01-01 00:00:00|work_mem|4MB|16MB|UPDATED')

  If both checks succeed, the task passes.
name: task_000000_18303bd3
